title: sv.messaging.respond_to.input
description: Input for sv.messaging.respond_to — a one-way message-delivery tool that CONTINUES an existing conversation. The platform delivers one message to the current routable participants of the conversation that `message_id` belongs to, minus you — the set shown as `participants` in the inbound envelope. You do NOT list recipients: the platform picks them (the conversation's roster), so continuing a conversation with everyone already on it is a single deterministic call instead of a reconstructed recipient list. The message stays in that same conversation, so it does not fork. Use sv.messaging.respond_to to respond to everyone already on a conversation; use sv.messaging.send to start a new conversation or address a different set, and sv.messaging.multicast to reach several recipients in independent 1-1 conversations. Because `message_id` is durable, sv.messaging.respond_to also continues that conversation later — keep a `message_id` and respond to it at any time. Delivery is one-way: the call returns a delivery acknowledgement; any response from a participant arrives later as a separate inbound message.

message_id.description: The id of a message you received, taken verbatim from an inbound envelope's `message_id`. The platform resolves it to its conversation and delivers to that conversation's current routable participants, minus you. It is a message id, not any internal handle — the platform identifies the conversation from it, and no internal conversation id ever crosses the tool boundary.

message.description: The message payload to deliver. Opaque to the platform; a JSON string or object. Must carry content: a non-empty string, or an object with a non-empty `content` (or `text` / `body`) field, or other structured fields. An empty or whitespace-only message is rejected — it would deliver an empty envelope the recipient reads as 'nothing was sent'. There is no empty acknowledgement; if you have nothing to add, do not respond.

reason.description: Optional rationale recorded verbatim alongside the delivery record. Never the model's hidden chain-of-thought.
